repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue Stat entry gives an interior continuous-dose fixed-dose minimax converse; stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap is policy-regret/overlap-decay and stat_sa_cate_pointwise was binary-treatment pointwise CATE, not continuous-dose partial means.

published_prior_art.cite: BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF gives the HOIF upper rate and explicitly says a tight lower bound remains elusive; KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2022MinimaxCATE gives fuzzy-hypothesis lower bounds for CATE, not continuous-dose density-normalized fixed-dose partial means.

The exponent comparison is implicit in BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF and classical pointwise nonparametric regression rates from Tsybakov2009Nonparametric, but is useful as a sanity reduction once the new converse is proved.

BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF flags uncertainty about whether treatment-density smoothness beta is structural or proof-artifactual; no verified comparator settles the beta<alpha phase diagram for this fixed-dose target.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The lower-bound claim is accepted only on the beta >= alpha, common-covariate-smoothness, interior fixed-dose Holder subclass, not as the full anisotropic phase diagram.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sharpness means matching the Bonvini-Kennedy HOIF upper rate on the stated beta >= alpha subclass.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:full-beta-frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The beta<alpha frontier is explicitly left open and is not part of the settled kernel.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The proposal advances with the full beta-frontier treated as a named open-ended follow-on rather than a claimed theorem.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: State the lower-bound construction route more concretely: list the pure-mu and coupled (mu,pi) subexperiments, bandwidth/amplitude calibration, chi-square or Hellinger control, and the density-normalization step needed to stay inside the continuous-dose class.

Add a corollary explicitly combining the new lower bound with the Bonvini-Kennedy upper theorem under matched assumptions, including any extra upper-side conditions needed for the HOIF rate so the sharpness claim has no assumption drift.
Update the anchor citation to the current Bonvini-Kennedy arXiv version and give exact section/equation anchors for the HOIF rate and the lower-bound-open-problem sentence.
Sharpen the beta<alpha open-ended question by naming two candidate phase forms or perturbation channels, so D0 has a concrete frontier-discrimination task rather than a broad phase-diagram prompt.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 on the field-tier beta >= alpha minimax converse, with the beta<alpha frontier kept as an open-ended follow-on.
